groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Minimum inputs for a legitimate, usable outreach draft.

groups.title: Campaign
groups.description: Goal, type, length, language, and ownership.

groups.title: Sender
groups.description: Real sender identity only — no impersonation.

groups.title: Sending identity & stack
groups.description: Domains, ESP, CRM, and reply ownership.

groups.title: Prospect
groups.description: Verified person and company facts only.

groups.title: Ideal customer profile
groups.description: Who belongs on the list — and who does not.

groups.title: Legal & privacy
groups.description: Jurisdiction, basis, and review status — do not invent compliance.

groups.title: Offer
groups.description: What you actually provide — no fake audits or free consults.

groups.title: Pricing & commercial
groups.description: Only confirmed prices, trials, and deadlines.

groups.title: Value proposition
groups.description: Clear outcome without hype or invented proof.

groups.title: Problem
groups.description: The business problem you are allowed to reference.

groups.title: Referrals & connections
groups.description: Only with permission and verified relationships.

groups.title: Customer evidence
groups.description: Approved case studies and metrics only.

groups.title: Reply handling
groups.description: How to classify and route replies.

groups.title: Human review
groups.description: What must be reviewed before send.

groups.title: A/B testing
groups.description: What may be tested and how.

variables.label: Primary Offer
variables.placeholder: Creatornivo Pro — structured AI content templates
variables.hint: What you are actually offering.

help.what: Primary offer presented in outreach.
help.why: The email must describe a real offer, not a bait-and-switch “audit.”
help.example: 30-minute product walkthrough of Creatornivo templates
help.avoid: Calling a sales call a free strategy consultation when it is not.

variables.label: Desired Action
variables.placeholder: Reply with a good time for a 15-minute call
variables.hint: Single primary next step for the recipient.

help.what: Primary desired action.
help.why: One clear, low-friction CTA improves reply quality.
help.example: Reply with a good time this week for 15 minutes
help.avoid: Multiple competing asks or high-friction CTAs on first touch.

variables.label: Job Title
variables.hint: Current verified title.

help.what: Prospect’s current job title.
help.why: Personalization and relevance depend on real role.
help.example: Head of Content
help.avoid: Guessing titles from old data or LinkedIn freeze.

variables.label: Company Name
variables.hint: Verified current employer.

help.what: Company Name — a parameter from the Cold Email Outreach brief (Prospect).
help.why: When provided, the model uses this instead of inventing prospect facts, compliance claims, or proof. Empty fields remain unset ([companyName]).
help.example: Acme Growth Co.
help.avoid: Invented prospect data, fake personalization, stolen lists, or compliance claims you have not confirmed for company name.

variables.label: Problem Addressed
variables.hint: Business problem you are allowed to reference.

help.what: The problem the outreach addresses.
help.why: Connects offer to prospect reality without inventing their pain.
help.example: Publishing cadence drops when the founder is the only writer
help.avoid: Claiming you know their private metrics or secrets.

variables.label: Tone
variables.hint: How the email should sound.

help.what: Tone of voice for the sequence.
help.why: Sets formality without sounding robotic or spammy.
help.example: direct, respectful, no hype
help.avoid: Fake urgency, flattery, or pressure language.

variables.label: Language
variables.hint: Language of the email package.

help.what: Language — a parameter from the Cold Email Outreach brief (Campaign).
help.why: When provided, the model uses this instead of inventing prospect facts, compliance claims, or proof. Empty fields remain unset ([language]).
help.example: English
help.avoid: Invented prospect data, fake personalization, stolen lists, or compliance claims you have not confirmed for language.

variables.label: Campaign Type
variables.hint: Pick one confirmed type — do not reframe later for “better open rates.”

help.what: The confirmed campaign type.
help.why: Structure, compliance expectations, and CTA style depend on type.
help.example: SaaS prospecting
help.avoid: Calling spam “B2B sales outreach” or changing type without reason.

options: reactivation of a previous business conversation
